channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Does the Queensland premier have a leash on his own attack dog?
item.description: Queensland's deputy premier made a 'dick' joke about his political opponent this week, but the difference in his approach and the premier's raises questions, writes political reporter Jack McKay.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Jarrod Bleijie's attacking approach differs from that taken by David Crisafulli.

item.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in DRC to 'very high'
item.description: The World Health Organization raises its assessment of the outbreak going nation-wide as a province in the Congo's east bans funerals and suspends the local football league.

media:group.media:description: Red Cross workers disinfect Rwampara's general hospital before handling a body on Thursday.

item.title: Dangerous area in Sydney for rock fishers trials life jacket library
item.description: Rock fishers in Sydney's east can now borrow and return life jackets for free in a bid to help reduce the number of drownings in the area.
item.dc:creator: Danuta Kozaki

media:group.media:description: Surf Life Saving NSW and Randwick City Council have established a free Life Jacket Library in Maroubra as part of SLNSW Rock Fishing Safety Project.

item.title: Shipping containers of water slide parts left idle as park plans axed
item.description: A developer blames the Iran war for his decision to axe a water park and resort project in regional Queensland despite already spending $21 million.
item.dc:creator: Adam Stephen and Sharnie Kim

media:group.media:description: Richard Cheong's home of 31 years backs onto an axed water park and resort development at Kewarra Beach in Cairns.

item.title: Confused about Labor's tax changes? Here's what they really mean
item.description: Labor's capital gains tax changes have set off a firestorm of debate about the implications for small businesses, start-ups, share owners and plenty more. Here's who will really pay more.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers set off a firestorm of debate with proposed tax changes.

item.title: Building townhouses on a residential Canberra block could soon be easier
item.description: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore and Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: The ACT government's proposed changes are aimed at increasing the number of terrace houses, townhouses, and low-rise apartments.

item.title: Rural women share harrowing IVF stories at parliamentary inquiry
item.description: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Blandin de Chalain, Lara Webster, and Brigitte Murphy

media:group.media:description: Sophie Fletcher has spent countless hours on the road travelling to metro centres to receive care.

item.title: 'Life is football' here but without money, Australia's most unique club won't survive
item.description: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.
item.dc:creator: Paul Kennedy and Paul Johnson

media:group.media:description: Willie Rioli Jr takes a breather on his return to Tiwi Bombers footy.

item.title: Fracking could open the door to 10 years of gas supply but is there a risk?
item.description: A decade's worth of gas supply for South Australia is potentially sitting below the Limestone Coast region. But will it ever be extracted?
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine and Becc Bird

media:group.media:description: Tony Beck opposes the lifting of the fracking moratorium on SA's Limestone Coast.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Donald Trump's director of national ​intelligence
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard says she is departing from the role to support her husband after his recent bone cancer diagnosis.

media:group.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard will step down from her role.

item.title: Digital tipping prompts on the rise but not all venue owners are fans
item.description: The peak hospitality industry group thinks electronic tipping is the way of the future, but some venue owners don't like pressuring customers to tip.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Willow McKenzie says the number of people who tip is "surprising".

item.title: ASIO assessment failed to identify heightened Hanukkah risks before Bondi attack
item.description: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.
item.dc:creator: Sean Rubinsztein-Dunlop

media:group.media:description: Fifteen people were killed in the attack at Bondi Beach on December 14.

item.title: Plastic waste collected in Sydney Harbour during Vivid festival rising
item.description: Data from floating rubbish bins near the Sydney CBD suggests there are higher levels of rubbish entering the water when major events such as Vivid are on.
item.dc:creator: Peter de Kruijff

media:group.media:description: A rainbow of plastic objects and wrappers are collected from Sydney Harbour every day.

item.title: The budget's central weakness was revealed this week
item.description: As debate over the federal government's budget tax hit on wealth and assets continues to reverberate, one can't help but wonder how it's going down among the gen Zs and millennials it's designed to impress.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Greber

media:group.media:description: For two elections in a row, Labor vowed to keep the current system of providing a 50 per cent discount on capital gains.

item.title: A new battleground has started for Israel, where words trump actions
item.description: In the wake of an outcry over its treatment of detained activists, Israel has focused on messaging as its actions undermine its support from the international community.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Benjamin Netanyahu said Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of flotilla activists was "not in line with Israel's values and norms".

item.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets
item.description: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.
item.dc:creator: Brett Worthington

media:group.media:description: Australian playwright Suzie Miller.

item.title: How a 3,400yo ball game survives in Mexico against all odds
item.description: Ulama remains an important part of Mexican identity, with authorities leveraging the momentum of the FIFA World Cup to shine a spotlight on the ancient sport once again.

media:group.media:description: The traditional Mesoamerican ballgame ulama is having a revival.

item.title: A community college had AI read out graduate names. It didn't go well
item.description: It's graduation season for universities across the US, which has bred an unexpected viral trend: graduates jeering at artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Videos of US graduates rejecting AI have been shared widely across social media.

item.title: 'How do you explain that?' The rare, complex case of PNG's conjoined twins
item.description: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.
item.dc:creator: Marian Faa

media:group.media:description: Sawong Kevin has become a star at the hospital where he receives care.

item.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry
item.description: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.
item.dc:creator: Kerry Staight

media:group.media:description: Agave plants can thrive in harsh conditions but their razor-sharp leaves make harvesting difficult and labour intensive.

item.title: 'Live-streamed genocide': Palestinian poet writes from Gaza's frontline
item.description: The month he turned 30, Mosab Abu Toha was blindfolded, beaten and detained by the Israel Defense Forces. The essay he wrote about it won a Pulitzer Prize.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Heath

media:group.media:description: Palestinian poet Mosab Abu Toha, pictured in front of the ruins of a building in Gaza, is appearing at the 2026 Sydney Writers' Festival.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Putin says at least six people killed in 'terrorist' attack on student dormitory
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has accused Ukraine of a "terrorist strike" on a student dormitory in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region that has killed at least six people and injured 39 others.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers at the scene of a destroyed dormitory building of the Starobilsk College.

item.title: Tax changes 'threaten to chill' record federal green energy support
item.description: As the federal government races towards its 2030 renewable energy goal, investors warn capital gains taxes changes risk upending the entire effort.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mercer

media:group.media:description: Wind projects in Australia are becoming increasingly hard to get over the line.

item.title: Tony Abbott returns to political life as Downer drops out of race for Liberal Party president
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott will be nominated unopposed to become the next federal president of the Liberal Party.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Tony Abbott will become the Liberal Party's president.
